title: IT Support Specialist
description:
Position: IT Support Specialist

    • Monday to Friday (8.30am - 5.00pm)
    • Office based role in SONAS HQ, Ballycoolin, Dublin 15


About SONAS Bathrooms:

SONAS is a leading brand and manufacturer of high-quality bathroom products. We design, manufacture, and distribute everyday luxury bathroom collections through a network of leading retail bathroom showrooms, builders' providers, and plumbers' merchants.

Our core values which guide our actions are Support, Openness, New Thinking, Ambition, and Sustainability. Our Vision is to be the brand that inspires you to reimagine bathrooms. Our Mission is to create long lasting, stylish and sustainable products, expertly crafted; and supported with exceptional customer service.

SONAS Bathrooms is an industry-leading organisation, recognised as one of Deloitte's Best Managed Companies every year since 2019.

Overview:

Sonas Bathrooms is seeking a proactive and technically capable IT Support Specialist to support and enhance our IT environment.

This role is responsible for day-to-day IT support while also contributing to the continuous improvement of digital business processes across the organisation. The successful candidate will support end users, infrastructure, and core systems, with particular exposure to SAP Business One on HANA, SUSE Linux, SQL databases, and system integrations.

Working closely with the IT Manager, you will help ensure stable operations today while supporting Sonas Bathrooms' longer-term digital transformation and automation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

End-User & Operational IT Support

    • Provide first- and second-level support to users across office, warehouse, and showroom environments.
    • Install, configure, and troubleshoot desktops, laptops, mobile devices, printers, and peripherals.
    • Manage and support user accounts, permissions, and access across Microsoft 365, Active Directory, and business systems.
    • Resolve support tickets efficiently while maintaining a high standard of service.


ERP & Core Business Systems

    • Provide first-line support for SAP Business One (HANA) users, including user setup, issue diagnosis, and functional assistance.
    • Assist with testing, validation, and rollout of system updates, enhancements, and integrations.
    • Support data accuracy, master data maintenance, and reporting requirements.
    • Liaise with external partners when escalation is required.


Linux, Database & Technical Systems

    • Support SUSE Linux Enterprise Server environments, including monitoring, patching, access control, and basic troubleshooting.
    • Assist with SAP HANA database operations such as backups, health checks, and performance monitoring.
    • Write, analyse, and troubleshoot SQL / SAP HANA SQL Script queries and views to support reporting and system analysis.
    • Assist in database housekeeping and system reliability tasks.


Infrastructure & Security

    • Support LAN/WAN networks, Wi-Fi, VPN access, and remote connectivity.
    • Assist with firewall, switch, and server administration under the direction of the IT Manager.
    • Monitor backups, endpoint protection, and system availability.
    • Support IT security best practices and compliance requirements.


Digital Processes & Continuous Improvement

    • Support and improve digital business processes across sales, warehouse, finance, and operations.
    • Assist in documenting as-is and to-be processes related to ERP usage, reporting, and integrations.
    • Identify opportunities to reduce manual effort, improve data quality, and streamline workflows.
    • Support the adoption of automation, system enhancements, and digital tools.
    • Ensure technology solutions align with business processes and operational needs.


Asset Management & Documentation

    • Maintain accurate IT asset, license, and warranty records.
    • Document procedures, configurations, and support processes.
    • Ensure IT standards and policies are consistently followed.


Requirements:

Education & Experience

    • Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
    • Minimum 1 year, (3+ an advantage) experience in IT support, systems administration, or ERP/application support.
    • College graduates considered if able to demonstrate knowledge and exposure in relation to the key responsibilities of the role. Aptitude and potential are essential factors.


Technical Skills

    • Operating Systems: Windows (desktop & server), Linux (SUSE preferred).
    • ERP: SAP Business One experience strongly preferred.
    • Databases: SQL and SAP HANA SQL Script (queries, views, troubleshooting).
    • Microsoft Stack: Active Directory, Microsoft 365 administration.
    • Networking: LAN/WAN, DNS, DHCP, VPN, firewall fundamentals.
    • Security & Backup: Endpoint protection, backup and recovery solutions.


Personal Competencies & Mindset

    • Strong communication and customer-focused approach.
    • Analytical and process-oriented way of thinking
    • Ability to understand how systems support business operations.
    • Organised, reliable, and able to manage multiple prioritie

title: Technical Support Engineer
description:
Technical Support Engineer – Precision Heating Ltd

About the Role

Precision Heating Ltd is a well-established and continually growing specialist provider of Heating, Ventilation and Renewable Energy Systems, operating across Ireland. We are the authorised agents for Viessmann in Ireland, responsible for the supply, technical support, servicing, and warranty of Viessmann products across the market.

We are now seeking a Technical Support Engineer to strengthen our internal technical function and provide high-quality technical support across our product range.

This is a technical, problem-solving role focused on supporting the sales team, customers, installers, and consultants with system selection, design assistance, and technical guidance. Sales involvement forms part of the role, but the primary focus is technical accuracy, suitability, and long-term system performance.

Comprehensive in-house training will be provided for the successful candidate.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

  • Providing technical support to the sales team on product selection, system design, and application
  • Advising customers, installers, contractors, and consultants on best-fit technical solutions
  • Assisting with technically accurate quotations, specifications, and system proposals
  • Reviewing drawings and project information to ensure systems are suitable and compliant
  • Handling inbound technical calls and enquiries in a professional, calm, and customer-focused manner
  • Acting as a technical point of contact for pre-sales and post-sales technical queries
  • Supporting bespoke heating and renewable energy system designs
  • Liaising with internal departments including service, commissioning, distribution, and management
  • Assisting with product and system training for internal teams and external stakeholders
  • Producing and maintaining technical support material and documentation as required

What We’re Looking For

We are looking for someone who:

  • Has a strong interest in heating and renewable energy systems
  • Enjoys technical problem-solving and supporting others with sound engineering advice
  • Is comfortable speaking with customers by phone and can communicate in a polite, clear, and professional manner
  • Can explain technical information clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Is methodical, organised, and detail-oriented
  • Takes ownership of technical enquiries and sees them through to resolution
  • Works well as part of a wider technical and sales support team

Experience & Training

  • Cert / Diploma in Building Services Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or similar is beneficial
  • Previous experience in the heating or building services industry is advantageous, but not essential
  • A strong technical mindset and willingness to learn is more important than prior experience
  • Full product, systems, and manufacturer training will be provided

Motivated candidates with the right attitude and an interest in developing their technical skills are strongly encouraged to apply.

Who This Role Suits

This role is well suited to someone who:

  • Enjoys providing technical support and solving engineering problems
  • Likes understanding how heating and renewable systems work in real-world applications
  • Is comfortable dealing with customers and technical professionals on a daily basis
  • Takes pride in accuracy, good engineering practice, and well-designed solutions
  • Is looking to grow within a technically focused and expanding company

This Role May Not Suit Someone Who

  • Is not comfortable handling technical queries or customer interaction by phone
  • Prefers minimal responsibility for technical accuracy or problem resolution
  • Is not interested in developing technical expertise over time

What Success Looks Like

  • Customers and internal teams receive clear, accurate, and professional technical support
  • Systems are correctly specified and appropriate for their application
  • Technical queries are handled efficiently and resolved to a high standard
  • Strong working relationships are built with customers, installers, and consultants

Why Join Precision Heating Ltd

  • Join a respected and continually growing specialist within the Irish heating and renewables sector
  • Be part of the authorised Viessmann agent team in Ireland
  • Receive structured training and ongoing technical development
  •  Work closely with experienced engineers and technical professionals
  • Clear opportunities for progression within the business as the company continues to grow

Job Type: Full-time
 Salary: €28,000 – €35,000 per year (depending on experience)

  • Benefits:
  • Bike to Work Scheme
  • Company Pension
  • On-site Parking

Schedule:
 Monday to Friday, 8am – 5pm

Work Location:
 Office-based – Blanchardstown, Dublin 15

Skills:
renewable energy Mechanical Engineering Service Delivery

title: EHS Graduate
description: About Aerogen: Headquartered in the vibrant city of Galway, Ireland, with offices around the world, Aerogen is proud to be the world leader in acute care aerosol drug delivery. Recognised through multiple MedTech awards, including being named Galway Chamber Company of the Year 2025, and an eleven-time winner of the Zenith Award for Respiratory Care Excellence in North America, our employees make a difference to patients’ lives every day, having reached over 30 million patients in more than 80 countries.Today, Aerogen is powered by a global team representing 54 nationalities, working together with a shared commitment to improving patient care. Our talented and skilled team collaborate to innovate, challenge and test not only in relation to our products but every facet of how we work.We lead the way by finding innovative solutions to even the most complex problems, all in the name of delivering better patient outcomes.Our business continues to grow rapidly across the globe and, as we grow, our core culture of “We Care” universally connects us. We believe that your ambition and integrity fuels ours, and we are committed to supporting our employees to reach their full potential through tangible investment in their careers.Join us as we continue to #discoverbetter. What is the role? The EHS Graduate will play a key supporting role within the Environmental, Health & Safety function, contributing to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of EHS systems across the organisation. This position will provide direct support to the wider EHS Team, with exposure to multi-site operations including Dangan, Parkmore and Shannon.The successful candidate will assist in driving EHS compliance, promoting a strong safety culture, and ensuring alignment with regulatory and corporate EHS standards.Responsibilities will include coordinating EHS administration, supporting risk management activities, facilitating training, and contributing to both site-level and global EHS initiatives.This role offers a strong development pathway, providing the graduate with hands-on practical experience across manufacturing environments and global EHS frameworks.What are the key responsibilities? • Support the implementation and continuous improvement of the company’s EHS Management System (14001/45001) across all sites.• Assist in maintaining the EHS Risk Assessment programme, ensuring hazards are identified, risks are assessed, and appropriate control measures are implemented and reviewed.• Coordinate and participate in scheduled EHS inspections and audits, ensuring timely close-out of findings and corrective actions.• Support accident and incident investigations, including root cause analysis and tracking of corrective and preventive actions (CAPAs).• Assist in the coordination, scheduling, and delivery of EHS training programmes, including new employee inductions and refresher training.• Collaborate with the Global EHS Lead and Senior EHS Specialist on the development, standardisation, and rollout of EHS policies, procedures, and global initiatives.• Contribute to the monitoring and reporting of EHS KPIs across sites, supporting data analysis and continuous improvement actions.• Engage with line managers and supervisors to promote a proactive safety culture, providing guidance and coaching where required.• Support cross-site EHS projects and initiatives, ensuring consistency and alignment with global standards.• Participate in regular EHS review meetings to track performance, progress, and priorities.• Assist with environmental management activities, including waste management, sustainability initiatives, and compliance obligations.• Provide support to facilities-related EHS requirements as needed across all sites.• Undertake additional responsibilities and project work as assigned by the Global EHS Lead.What education and experience are required? • Degree (or pending) in Environmental Health & Safety, Environmental Science, Occupational Health & Safety, or a related discipline.• Strong understanding of EHS principles, legislation, and best practices, ideally within a manufacturing or industrial environment.• Previous internship, placement, or project experience in EHS is advantageous.• Demonstrated ability to analyse problems, identify root causes, and support effective solutions.• Knowledge & Understanding of ISO 14001 (Environmental Management) & 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety Management) Standards.What key skills will make you great at the role? • Working knowledge of occupational health, safety, and environmental regulations.• Understanding of risk assessment methodologies and hazard identification.• Awareness of environmental management practices, including waste, energy, water and sustainability (desirable).• High level of integrity and confidentiality.• Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and engage stakeholders at all levels.• Proactive, self-motivated, and capable of working both independently and as part of a team.• Excellent organisational and time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities.• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.• Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).• Flexibility and willingness to travel between Dangan, Parkmore and Shannon sites as required.• Adaptability to work within a dynamic, fast-paced, and evolving environment.What is it like to work at Aerogen?Our purpose is to transform patient lives and our ethos is to #discoverbetter. This also translates to how we look after our people. We want talented, passionate and engaged people to join Aerogen and build your career with us. We understand that your life evolves through different stages, and we are here to support our global team with a range of financial and non-financial rewards and benefits. There’s something for everyone! Here is an idea of what we offer:• Excellent medical care.• Bonus & Pension.• ‘

title: Graduate sales & account manager
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.In this six-month internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth.A few examples of your responsibilitiesHelp build a world-class B2B sales function, contributing to the strategy, processes, and standards that shape how we approach enterprise customers.Identify and prioritize high-potential leads using data and the latest AI tools, then convert corporate clients through cold calls and targeted outreach to drive long-term revenue growth.Develop a deep understanding of your industry’s incentives, decision-makers, and purchasing dynamics to build strong, long-term client relationships.Represent our products with clarity and confidence, collecting feedback from clients and sharing actionable insights to help shape product development.Use performance data to understand what works, focus on the highest-value opportunities, and continuously refine your sales approach.Deliver exceptional service at every touchpoint, building trust and long-term partnerships with clients.What we look forReasoning ability. Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.Drive. You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.Team spirit. You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.Proficiency in English. You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.What we offerIncredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams. You’ll work in small, result-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.An exceptional opportunity for growth. We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.Competitive pay and access to equity in the company. Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £56,345 in London and €54,346 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £68,579 and £151,436 in London, and €66,065 and €145,430 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.All. These. Benefits. Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top-of-the-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.Commitment & contractPermanent or fixed-term. Full-time or part-time.LocationMilan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.The selection processIn our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self-reject.If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.Before you applyIf you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learnin

title: Medical Scientist- Haematology
description: Post Title: Medical Scientist, Haematology Post Status: Permanent Department: Haematology Location: Beaumont Hospital & Affiliated Sites Reports to: Chief Medical Scientist Salary: Appointment will be made on the Medical Scientist Scale (€44,331 - € 72,268, LSI1 €73,667) at a point in line with Government pay policy Hours of work: Full-Time, 35 hours per week Closing Date: 12 Noon on 06/05/2026 Please note the hospital reserves the right to close the competition early should a substantial number of applications be received. Responsibilities Principal Duties & Responsibilities:  To contribute to the provision of a high quality patient focused service.  To perform such technical duties as directed by the Chief Medical Scientist or deputy.  To assist in training Medical Laboratory Assistants, support staff and visiting workers as required.  To contribute to maintaining department accreditation with INAB through compliance with ISO15189 standards  To assist in the training of new and existing staff.  To cooperate with and support all project work undertaken in the Laboratory Directorate.  To assist in implementing measures for Safety, Health and Welfare to all staff.  To assist in providing statistical and management information when requested.  To attend courses and meetings as appropriate to maintain up-to-date knowledge.  To fulfil CPD requirements.  Any other duties as appropriate to the grade and qualifications.  To actively participate in external and internal quality assurances schemes.  To participate in the Directorate Joint Review Process The job description is an outline of the post as it is currently perceived by the hospital and will be subject to review and amendment. This job description will therefore be subject to change in light of the various strategies for the hospital and the development of the Clinical Directorate model and any future developments that arise and will evolve and change over time.Qualifications Selection Criteria: Selection criteria outline the qualifications, skills, knowledge and/or experience that the successful candidate would need to demonstrate for successful discharge of the responsibilities of the post.Applications will be assessed on the basis of how well candidates satisfy these criteria. Mandatory:  Statutory Registration, Professional Qualifications, Experience, etc  Candidates for appointment must:  Be registered, or be eligible for registration, on the Medical Scientists Register maintained by the Medical Scientists Registration Board at CORU. OR  (ii)  Applicants who satisfy the conditions set out in Section 91 of the Health and Social Care Professionals Act 2005, (see note 1 below*), must submit proof of application for registration with the Medical Scientists Registration Board at CORU.The acceptable proof is correspondence from the Medical Scientists RegistrationBoard at CORU confirming their application for registration as a Section 91 applicant was received by the 30th March 2021. AND  (iii)  Have the requisite knowledge and ability (including a high standard of suitability and professional ability) for the proper discharge of the duties of the office. AND  (iv)  Provide proof of Statutory Registration on the Medical Scientists Register maintained by the Medical Scientists Registration Board at CORU before a contract of employment can be issued. Applicable to Section 38 applicants only). Annual registration * (Applicable to Section 38 Applicants only*)  On appointment practitioners must maintain annual registration on the MedicalScientists Register maintained by the Medical Scientists Registration Board atCORU. AND  (ii)  Practitioners must confirm annual registration with CORU to the HSE by way of the annual Patient Safety Assurance Certificate (PSAC). Health Candidates for and any person holding the office must be fully competent and capable of undertaking the duties attached to the office and be in a state of health such as would indicate a reasonable prospect of ability to render regular and efficient service. Character Candidates for and any person holding the office must be of good character.Note 1*Section 91 candidates are individuals who qualified before 31st March 2019 and have been engaged in the practice of the profession in the Republic of Ireland for a minimum of 2 years fulltime (or an aggregate of 2 years fulltime), between 31st March 2014 and 31st March 2019 are considered to be Section 91 applicants under the Health and Social Care Professionals Act 2005.Note 2*For information in relation to the type of applicants – please click on this link:https://coru.ie/health-and-social-care-professionals/registration/what-kind-of-applicant-am-i-/ Please Note, Students due to Graduate (CORU Approved Courses) are encouraged to apply.  Supplementary information:  The Hospital  www.beaumont.ie  Management Unit: www.beaumont.ie/hr Other ( Please specify ):  Informal Enquiries ONLY to:  (Please note NO APPLICATIONS will be accepted via the Informal Email)  Name: Karen Foley Title: Chief Medical Scientist Email address: karenfoley2@beaumont.ieIf an appointee has previously been employed in the Civil or Public Service and that appointee is entitled to or in receipt of a pension from the Civil or Public Service or where a Civil/ Public Service pension comes into payment during the appointee’s re-employment that pension will be subject to abatement in accordance with Section 52 of the Public Service Pensions (Single Scheme and other Provisions) Act 2012. Please note: In applying for this position you are acknowledging that you understand that the abatement provisions, where relevant, will apply. It is not envisaged that the employing Department/ Office/ Body will support an application for an abatement waiver in respect of appointments to this position A short listing exercise may be carried out on the basis of information supplied in your application. The criteria for ranking and or short listing are based on the requirements of the post

title: AI Research & Automation Developer
description:
About Us

Glancy Enviro is one of Ireland’s leading environmental services providers, with over 20 years of experience delivering high-quality drainage, CCTV surveying, pest control, and environmental solutions.

We are proud to be a Great Place to Work, fostering a culture built on teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement.

We are continuing to invest in technology, systems and AI to improve how we work, support our customers, and create better processes across the business.

We are now recruiting an AI Research & Automation Developer to join our office team in Longford.

This is a new and exciting role for someone who is passionate about AI, automation, systems, research and practical problem solving.

The Role

The successful candidate will research, test and help implement AI tools and automation solutions across the business.

This role is not just about theory. We are looking for someone who can identify real business problems, test suitable AI tools, build practical solutions, document processes, and support the rollout of new systems across our office, sales, operations and customer service teams.

You will work closely with management and different departments to find ways AI can save time, improve customer service, reduce admin, support reporting, and improve decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Research and test AI tools relevant to the business
  • Build and improve AI-assisted workflows for office, sales, operations and customer service teams
  • Support the development of AI knowledge bases for internal teams and customer-facing agents
  • Assist with AI voice agent and customer service automation projects
  • Create prompts, workflows, templates and process documents
  • Review existing manual processes and identify automation opportunities
  • Work with systems such as CRM, phone systems, spreadsheets, forms, documents and reporting tools
  • Help connect systems using automation platforms, APIs or integrations where possible
  • Test new tools before rollout and document results clearly
  • Train and support staff on new AI tools and workflows
  • Monitor AI outputs for accuracy, quality and usefulness
  • Keep up to date with new AI tools, trends and business applications
  • Ensure AI use is practical, safe, secure and appropriate for business use

Suitable Experience

The ideal candidate may come from one of the following backgrounds:

  • AI tools / automation
  • Software development
  • Business systems
  • Data analysis
  • IT support with automation experience
  • Process improvement
  • CRM or workflow automation
  • Recent graduate with strong AI, coding or systems ability

Skills We Are Looking For

  • Strong interest in AI and automation
  • Good research and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to test tools and explain findings clearly
  • Strong computer and systems ability
  • Good understanding of business processes
  • Comfortable working with spreadsheets, documents, forms and digital systems
  • Experience with tools such as ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Make, Zapier, Power Automate, n8n, APIs, Python or similar would be an advantage
  • Ability to document processes clearly
  • Good communication skills
  • Practical mindset — able to turn ideas into working solutions
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Ability to work independently and manage projects

Desirable but Not Essential

  • Experience with Salesforce or other CRM systems
  • Experience with Aircall or phone system integrations
  • Experience using APIs
  • Basic coding experience, particularly Python or JavaScript
  • Experience creating dashboards or reports
  • Experience with AI agents, chatbots or voice agents
  • Understanding of data protection and GDPR

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: €40,000.00-€50,000.00 per year

Benefits:

  • Company events
  • Employee assistance program
  • On-site parking

Language:

  • English (preferred)

Work Location: In person

title: Document Controller
description:
About the Role


ORS are seeking a Document Controller or Administrator to join our Health & Safety Management Team.

This role is responsible for the effective drafting, control, coordination and management of Health & Safety and PSDP Process documentation across multiple projects, ensuring accuracy, compliance and timely availability of records.

Key Responsibilities


  • Draft, control, manage, and maintain Health & Safety and PSDP documentation, required across multiple construction projects and operations.
  • Support the preparation and ongoing maintenance of project documentation, including Preliminary Safety & Health Plans and Stage Reports
  • Support the preparation and maintenance of Team documentation to inc. Fee bids, Tenders and General Administrative requirements
  • Assist in the preparation, review and handover of project Safety Files requiring coordination with design teams, contractors and project PSCS
  • Liaise with project teams, designers, contractors, and external stakeholders regarding documentation requirements
  • Ensure version control, document numbering, approvals and distribution protocols are followed
  • Maintain document registers and ensure records are accurate, complete, and auditable
  • Support internal and external audits related to Health & Safety documentation
    • Maintain confidentiality and data integrity of all documentation in line with GDPR requirements


Skills & Experience


  • Proven experience in a Document Controller/ Administrative, or similar role, within Health & Safety, construction, engineering or regulated environments
  • Experience in the compilation of PSDP documentation and Safety Files is highly desirable
  • Strong organisational skills with excellent attention to detail
  • Experience managing controlled documents and document management systems
  • Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, SharePoint or similar)
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines
    • Strong communication and coordination skills

What We Can Offer You

ORS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive company culture. We foster a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects and supports their individual strengths and experiences.

Being part of ORS means more than just a job, it means joining a company that invests in you and your ambitions with transparency, structure and support.

We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join ORS, which include:

  • Competitive salary
  • Performance-related bonus
  • Pension scheme with matching contributions
  • Remote-first hybrid model – use your home as a base and any of our office locations as you see fit
  • Work from abroad
  • Flexible working hours
  • Access to over 60 health disciplines through our Kara Connect Platform
  • Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme & funding
  • Paid maternity, paternity and fertility leave
  • Company and birthday annual leave days
  • Death in Service benefit
  • Tax saver - travel and bike to work
  • Culture, social, wellness and CPD committees
  • Talent Development Programmes
Access to leading career coach Gerry Duffy

About ORS

ORS is a world-class multidisciplinary building consultancy with more than 30 years of delivering technical excellence across the built environment. Our mission is simple but powerful: We endeavour to make the lives of our team, clients, partners and communities happier and better in the ways that really matter every single day.

Don’t just take our word for it! ORS has won numerous awards, recognising our Great Place to Work culture.

  • Winner of the Engineering Consultancy of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025
  • Winner of the Graduate Programme of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025
  • Winner of the Best Company to Work For at the Irish Design & Build Awards 2024 & 2025
  • Winner of the Best People Development of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2024
  • Winner of the Recruitment & Retention Strategy at the Workplace Excellence Awards 2024
  • Great Place to Work certified consecutively since 2017
  • Top 3 Company Best Workplaces in the medium-sized category at the Great Place to Work awards 2025
  • Best Workplaces for Health & Wellbeing 2025 & 2026 Great Place to Work
  • Engineers Ireland CPD Accreditation since 20217
  • Awarded for Hybrid Way of Working at the 2022 Great Place to Work Awards
  • Silver Accreditation through Investors in Diversity 2025

title: Graduate Environmental Consultant
description:
Overview

Shape a sustainable tomorrow.

Do you want to join a team that asks questions and dares to see things differently?

We are AtkinsRéalis, a world-class engineering services and nuclear organization. When you join us, you'll help create world-class sustainable solutions that connect people, data, and technology. We're transforming infrastructure and energy systems, growing partnerships with our clients, and bringing the most complex projects across the globe to life. Join us and engineer a better future for our planet and its people.

Our global team of 36,000+ employees speak over 70 languages and represents 130 nationalities across six continents. Our work covers a variety of sectors, including transportation, infrastructure, environment, water, energy, defence, technology and aerospace.

On our Graduate Development programme, you take the driver's seat in your career. Grow personally and professionally, guided by inspiring managers. Say yes to opportunities that spark your interest, knowing you'll have training and the support you need to maintain a healthy work-life balance.

Reaching your full potential means being able to bring your whole self to work and feeling like you belong.

Where could you work

As a Graduate environmental engineer, you'll enjoy variety, working on large and small projects with the opportunity to explore different career pathways, including consulting and design. You'll be a part of our vibrant and expanding multidisciplinary team. We're the gateway to planning, urban design and Environmental Impact Assessment within the organisation, comprising a vibrant network of people providing world-class consultancy and advisory services.

We're committed to providing the best outcomes for our clients, always with sustainability in mind. We share our understanding of the unprecedented challenges they face – climate change, rapid urbanisation, biodiversity decline, consumerisation, economic uncertainty, security threats and technology-driven change – to create a better future. The work we do is important and influential, it shapes the way our clients think, enables them to make better informed choices and deliver their projects effectively in a world of disruption and change.

As a graduate, you'll have plenty of opportunities to be involved in a variety of projects, ranging from energy and renewables, water, nuclear, property, rail, highways, international projects, planning and stakeholder engagement.

Your role

  • Supporting with the coordination of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) and other non-statutory environmental appraisals.
  • Supporting with coordinating a multi-disciplinary team of environmental specialists inputting to environmental assessments and environmental design inputs.
  • Collecting and analysing data including conducting field surveys where required.
  • Carrying out desk-based baseline constraints studies and appraisals.
  • Writing factual reports and sharing findings with multi-disciplinary colleagues, clients, sub-contractors and/or regulators or other stakeholders.
  • Supporting Senior environmental engineers / consultants, as required, in the delivery of robust planning documentation, to required best practice standards, in accordance with an agreed scope of work, and to the agreed delivery programme.
  • Working with colleagues, clients, environmental specialists and engineering teams to help promote sustainable design solutions and environmental enhancement.

About you

  • Minimum Level 8 degree in Earth Science, Environmental Assessment & Management, Environmental Geoscience, Environmental Impact Assessment, Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geography or Sustainability.
  • A passion for working in a consultancy environment.
  • Championing a culture where everyone belongs.
  • A desire to work collaboratively with a curious approach, challenging yourself to find new solutions.
  • Enthusiasm and drive to develop your skillset and seek out development opportunities within a supportive team.
  • Motivation to achieve professional accreditation as your career progresses (where applicable).
  • Comfortably embrace agile ways of working and learning new technologies.
  • Commitment to spend a minimum of three days a week in the office or on client site (project dependent). Being together in person helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more effectively, and accelerate your learning and development.

Training and development

Our graduate development programme is designed to give you an insight into many areas of work, and the chance to explore where your own skills and interests lie. It features a series of training events and modules, professional development, mentoring and on-the-job experience as well as practical skills development.

From day one, you'll work on real projects that offer scale and variety, collaborating with colleagues and creating meaningful impact on the world around us. You'll work towards your professional accreditation (where applicable) - whichever path you take, your future starts here.

We're proud to have been an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited employer since 2021. This means you'll be supported by a true learning and development organization dedicated to your future.

We can advise on the next deadline for applications (previous deadline was Sunday, 4th January 2026). As our graduate development programmes are incredibly popular we recommend that you apply early as some roles may close sooner.

Rewards & benefits

Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at

title: Assistant Heritage Consultant - 2026 Graduate Programme
description:
See it differently

You see the world through a unique lens and that’s exactly what we need. We embrace how you see things differently – it’s essential for creating a sustainable future.

Arup is a global consultancy with advisory and technical expertise across more than 150 disciplines. We guide, plan and design the future of the built environment. Globally, each year we recruit hundreds of graduates to help us see things differently and solve the most complex challenges facing our clients, climate and communities.

The Opportunity

The heritage element of our Environmental Planning team is newly established but gathering momentum. As such, we are seeking an Assistant Heritage Consultant to join the team, based at One Albert Quay in Cork. This is a full-time permanent role. Our projects are located throughout the country, and the role will require occasional travel to sites and other Arup offices.

This is an entry level role, suitable for recent graduates or people early in their careers.

The role will include conducting research, writing Archaeological Impact Assessments, Architectural Heritage Impact Assessments, undertaking site and archive visits, and contributing to Environmental Impact Assessment Report cultural heritage chapters. The role will also include using GIS systems to supplement our assessments. You will be expected to work with colleagues across a wide range of disciplines to achieve project objectives.

At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective – in which we encourage individuality to thrive. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives and ideas. You will have the opportunity do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, to our members and to the clients and communities we serve.

Is this role right for you?

We are looking for people with:

Essential

  • A Bachelor's degree in archaeology (joint honours or minor degree acceptable)
  • Research skills and a high level of written expression
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Desirable

  • A Master's degree in archaeology / architectural heritage related subjects
  • Some knowledge of the Irish legislative framework and policy and its practical application to projects affecting the historic environment
  • Knowledge / experience of undertaking desk-based assessments, heritage impact assessments, or similar assessments
  • Proficiency in GIS (ArcGIS preferred) and dataset manipulation
  • A full driving licence

Please submit a copy of your CV and Transcripts when submitting an application



What we offer you

Beyond a great salary and stimulating, technically challenging work, Arup offers a range of additional benefits to its people.

As a membership organisation, everyone shares in our success through a global profit share scheme. Our members also have access to a range of broader wellbeing benefits. You’ll have access to lots of learning and development opportunities. This includes a structured induction and ongoing training through Arup university.

Additionally, you'll have the opportunity to utilise your technical expertise to do meaningful work that strengthens the resilience of communities and places to improve lives through Arup's community engagement programme.

Our hybrid work model creates an environment that accommodates different needs and allows members to integrate their work and personal lives alongside the needs of our business, clients and communities.

Next steps

complete the application form, attaching any required documents. We will be reviewing applications for this position on a rolling basis. You are therefore strongly encouraged to apply in advance of the application deadline.

Different people, shared values

At Arup, we encourage individuality to thrive by respecting, sharing and connecting our diverse experiences, perspectives and ideas so we can grow together.

At Arup we are an equal opportunity employer, and we accept people for who they are – we encourage applications from people of all backgrounds regardless of age, gender identity and expression, marital status, disability, neurodiversity and mental health, race, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. Your difference belongs here. We are an open environment that embraces diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas – this drives our excellence.

Our internal employee networks support our inclusive culture – from race, ethnicity and cross-cultural working to gender, LGBTQ+ and disability – we aim to create a space for you to express yourself and make a positive difference - Discover more about life at Arup at www.arup.com/careers/your-life-at-arup.

Please contact UKEarlyCareersRecruitment@arup.com to let us know if you need any assistance or reasonable adjustments throughout your application and we will do everything we possibly can to support you. description:

You see the world through a unique lens and that’s exactly what we need. We embrace how you see things differently – it’s essential for creating a sustainable future.

The Graduate Role in Operations, Risk, Compliance and Resilience is designed for people interested in contributing to better outcomes for our clients' operational processes and digital systems.

  • Time management skills to meet project deadlines.

  • Adaptability and eagerness to learn and develop professionally.

title: Social Media Content Creator
description:
About The Role

Seed Golf is looking for a Social Media & Content Creator to help bring our brand to life across digital platforms, shoots, and real-world experiences.

This role sits at the intersection of content, storytelling, and execution. You are the person who ensures what we do — on the ground, on the course, and behind the scenes — actually shows up in the world in a way that feels sharp, authentic, and worth paying attention to.

This role requires you to be great at two things:
Creating and publishing engaging, social-first content that reflects the Seed voice
Supporting content production and logistics so shoots, trips, and campaigns run smoothly

You’ll work closely with the founder, marketing team, and external collaborators. You’ll also get a front-row seat to how a modern golf brand is built — without the usual industry fluff.

Attitude, taste, and follow-through matter just as much as experience.

What You’ll Do

Day-to-Day

Own day-to-day social execution (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, etc.)
Write and publish posts, captions, and stories in the Seed voice
Create short-form content (Reels, TikTok, behind-the-scenes, product storytelling)
Engage with the community (comments, DMs, conversations)
Stay plugged into golf, sport, and culture to inform what we create
Help manage content calendars and campaign rollouts

Content & Production

Support shoots (planning, coordination, execution)
Capture content on shoots: BTS, short-form, social-first moments
Help turn shoots into multiple outputs — not just one asset
Work with editors and creatives to ensure content lands properly
Assist with logistics: scheduling, travel, locations, basic coordination

On the Road

Travel to shoots, events, and brand activations
Capture real-time content that feels immediate and relevant
Be adaptable when plans change (because they always do)
Help ensure we get the content we need — without overcomplicating the process

What Success Looks Like

Social channels feel alive, consistent, and unmistakably Seed
Content feels real, not overproduced or generic
Shoots run smoothly and actually result in usable content
We capture moments that connect — not just content for the sake of it

What We’re Looking For

Strong understanding of Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube
A clear sense of taste — what’s good, what’s not, and why
Ability to shoot and create content (phone is fine — gear is a bonus)
Strong writing skills (captions, hooks, short-form scripts)
Highly organized and reliable
Comfortable juggling creative and logistical responsibilities
Calm, adaptable, and resourceful under pressure
Confident communicator who can work with internal teams and external partners
Willingness to travel and be on the move

Bonus Points

Experience in golf, sport, or lifestyle brands
Basic editing skills (CapCut, Premiere, etc.)
Understanding of DTC brands and storytelling
Appreciation for doing more with less

Who This Role Is Not For

Someone who only wants to edit
Someone focused purely on growing their own personal brand
Someone who needs rigid structure or constant direction
Someone not comfortable with travel, unpredictability, or getting stuck in

Why This Role Matters

Seed exists to challenge the way golf is marketed and experienced.

Your job is to help us show that — daily.

You’ll shape how people see the brand, how they connect with it, and ultimately how they experience the game through us.

About Seed Golf

Seed Golf was founded by Dean Klatt, a 25-year golf industry veteran who got tired of the marketing BS.

We make high-performance golf balls — without the inflated prices — and build a brand focused on making the game more accessible, more honest, and more enjoyable.

No gimmicks. No noise. Just better golf.

title: Logistics and Hardware Operations Analyst - Manufacturing and Distribution -9 months contract
description:
Job Description


What you will do


  • The Operations Analyst will be responsible for monitoring and processing of daily hardware requests with accuracy in a timely manner.

  • Perform hardware receiving, inspection, put away and cycle counting to maintain inventory accuracy.

  • Organize inventory and handle variety of daily operations inquiries and issues. Assist team with hardware devices configurations for laptops, network equipment, tokens, etc.

  • Perform hardware setup and installation, deliver hardware to internal network sites, and participate in projects as dictated by the business need.

  • Review and update procedures to ensure accuracy to meet business requirements. Keep lab & associated work areas clean, safe and follow all safety guidelines, and consistently demonstrates and incorporates principals of safety for self and others into daily activities.

  • Identify areas for process improvement, cost savings, and collaborate with associates to improve efficiency, productivity, and processes.

  • Communicate to management team regularly operational status and provide insights on performance trends as well as recommendations on how to exceed plan and accelerate productivity and growth.

  • Perform transactions, maintain accurate system data, analysing and creating operational reports, and data entry and system transactions using Oracle applications and provide daily status and reports to management.

  • Troubleshoot basic issues independently and report issues to management. May travel to assist network hardware installation or removals.

  • Work involves some problem solving with assistance and guidance in understanding and applying company policies and procedures.

  • Ship hardware to end user or destination, in compliance with Oracle Policies and Procedures. Familiarly with Parcel Carrier Procedures, Systems and Transportation Networks essential.

  • Responsible for shipping, receiving, storing, and distributing Computer Hardware, Peripherals and related equipment. Includes material planning and scheduling.

  • Meets with suppliers to ensure highest quality Hardware deliveries within established lead times.

  • The Operations Analyst is an essential role and will report to our Oracle M&D office daily. This position does not offer teleworker or remote/working from home options.


Desired Experience


  • BA/BS degree, typically graduate or equivalent with 2 years’ experience.

  • Knowledge of desktop support / PC repair and hardware configuration a plus.

  • Knowledge of Logistics and Hardware Distribution methodologies.

  • Knowledge of Microsoft Office.

  • Good computer and technical skills; quick to learn new tools applicable to the role.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

  • Willingness to work in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

  • Strong customer services skills with the ability to maintain professionalism under pressure.

  • Exhibits strong organizational skills, attention to detail and follow-through. Ability to multitask in a dynamic environment.

  • Analytical skills and ability to meet deadlines.

  • Ability to lift, carry, push, or pull up to 50 lbs and drive company vehicles.


What we will offer you


  • A competitive salary with exciting benefits

  • Learning and development opportunities to advance your career

  • An Employee Assistance Program to support your mental health

  • An inclusive culture that celebrates what makes you unique


At Oracle, we don’t just respect differences—we celebrate them. We believe that innovation starts with inclusion and to create the future we need people with diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. That’s why we’re committed to creating a workplace where all kinds of people can do their best work. It’s when everyone’s voice is heard and valued that we’re inspired to go beyond what’s been done before.

About Us


As a world leader in cloud solutions, Oracle uses tomorrow’s technology to tackle today’s challenges. We’ve partnered with industry-leaders in almost every sector—and continue to thrive after 40+ years of change by operating with integrity.


We know that true innovation starts when everyone is empowered to contribute. That’s why we’re committed to growing an inclusive workforce that promotes opportunities for all.


Oracle careers open the door to global opportunities where work-life balance flourishes. title: SAP Application Support Engineer
description:

title: First Line Support Technician for On-Bus Systems
description:
First Line Support Technician for On-Bus Systems

Bus Éireann is looking for practical, hands-on individuals to join our operational teams in Dublin, Galway, Limerick and Waterford.

In this role, you will help keep our buses running smoothly each day by checking, supporting and looking after key onboard equipment. This includes ticket machines and other systems such as location tracking, communication devices, CCTV, WiFi and similar onboard equipment. A 5 over 7 day working arrangement starts at 5am to support daily run out, and typically includes one Sunday in every 4 to carry out health checks, updates and equipment audits.

As part of a local team, your main role is to make sure each bus leaves the depot with a working ticket machine and that other onboard systems are regularly checked and functioning properly. You will work closely with local depot staff, central support teams, suppliers and other operations teams to quickly resolve any issues that arise.

This role requires confidence using technology, keeping accurate records and keeping track of equipment performance. You will work side-by-side with teams such as Maintenance, IT, Cash Office, local operations and AVL (vehicle tracking) to ensure smooth daily operations.


Key Responsibilities:

Frontline Support

  • Be the first point of contact for depot staff who report issues with onboard equipment.

  • Carry out basic checks and troubleshooting, such as restarting devices, updating settings and reviewing equipment status.

  • Log issues accurately in dedicated systems and escalate them when needed.

  • Help drivers and depot staff with quick fixes to avoid delays to service.

  • Check and repair ticket machines where possible and carry out simple functionality tests.

  • Replace faulty ticketing equipment or SIM cards when required, maintaining accurate records.

  • Keep track of faulty equipment, label it and prepare it for collection or repair by suppliers.

  • Test new software updates on onboard devices and carry out resets if required.

Monitoring and Routine Checks

  • Monitor system dashboards and ServiceNow alerts to spot equipment issues early.

  • Highlight repeated or ongoing problems to the appropriate teams.

  • Assist with software updates and regular equipment checks.

  • Replace faulty onboard plug out/plug in equipment using the spare stock available, ensuring the correct settings and information are installed.

  • Complete and record regular health checks on onboard systems, such as connectivity, log-ons and software versions.

  • Ensure spare equipment is available and updated daily so buses can be deployed without delay.

  • Check ticket machines download the latest information and upload revenue data each day.

  • Work with station teams to make sure enough ticketing equipment is available and faulty items are returned for repair.

Record Keeping and Reporting

  • Keep an up-to-date record of all onboard equipment across your region using dedicated apps and ServiceNow email.

  • Maintain logs of faulty items, replacements and where each piece of equipment is located.

  • Track spare equipment levels and arrange replenishments when needed.

  • Produce periodic reports showing:

  • Equipment performance and issues.

  • Percentage of buses fully operational.

  • Trends or repeated issues across the fleet.

  • Up-to-date equipment lists and stock levels.

Communication and Teamwork

  • Work closely with local operations teams, AVL Control, Traffic Supervisors and Cash Office staff.

  • Coordinate with Maintenance to report equipment problems linked to wiring, power or mounting plates.

  • Collaborate with central support teams, IT, Maintenance Supervisors and supplier technicians to resolve issues and carry out updates or repairs.

  • Work with the NTA and their contractors to support NTA-managed onboard systems.

  • Provide clear notes and explanations when passing issues to other teams.

Key Skills & Knowledge

  • Practical ability to carry out basic checks, troubleshooting and simple repairs on onboard equipment, such as power, connections and simple diagnostics.

  • Comfortable using computers, laptops and software tools to update, check and record equipment status.

  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to work through issues methodically and escalate when needed.

  • Good organisation and record-keeping skills, including maintaining logs, tracking equipment and managing spare stock.

  • Clear and confident communicator with the ability to support drivers, depot staff and other operational teams.

  • Reliable, proactive and capable of working independently while managing daily responsibilities.

  • Full, clean Category B driving licence, with flexibility to travel between depots.

  • Experience in technical support, transport operations or working with equipment or devices is an advantage.

  • A qualification in electrical, mechanical or a similar practical/technical area is an advantage.


Why Join Bus Éireann?

At Bus Éireann, you’ll be part of a team that values people, collaboration and continuous improvement. This role offers the chance to make a real impact by supporting our workforce, shaping a positive employee experience and helping to make Bus Éireann a great place to work.

We offer a supportive and

title: ICT Support Engineer - Level 3
description:
PFH Technology are currently looking for an ICT Support Engineer - Level 3 to join our growing team.


Location: Dublin 1, hybrid

Contract length: 12-months (with expected extensions)


Responsibilities:

The ideal candidate will have at least 10-years’ experience in the following:


  • Configure and manage Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for identity governance, conditional access, and MFA.
  • Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and role-based access controls.
  • Deploy and manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, application deployment, and endpoint configuration.
  • Configure & manage Microsoft Defender XDR solutions (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365 etc).
  • Monitor and respond to security alerts using Microsoft Sentinel and other SIEM tools.
  • Apply best practices for securing Azure resources, including network security groups, firewalls, and encryption.
  • Implement Zero Trust principles across identity, devices, and applications.
  • Lead investigations for security incidents and provide remediation strategies.
  • Maintain detailed documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures.
  • Must be able to show a high degree of awareness of cybersecurity best practices and concepts including phishing awareness, Identity and Access Management, Multifactor Authentication, Zero Trust Security Model, Purview specially Information Protection
  • Must have demonstrated experience of threat evaluation and security incident response
  • Must have exposure to and be able to demonstrate experience using an ITIL aligned Service desk and associated principles such as Incident and Service request management, Problem Management, Change Management, continual Service improvement etc.
  • Must be able to demonstrate excellent support & troubleshooting skills for Level 3 Incident Management aligned with ITIL best practices (Incident management, Service Request and Problem Management).

Duties:

  • Be willing and capable to work well within a team environment
  • Be able to clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner demonstrating a customer service mind-set
  • Provide third line support for ICT Operations and other ICT Teams
  • Liaise with other ICT technical team specialists within the tenderer’s organisation to complete projects, (the response should clearly detail the areas where the tenderer has technical expertise).
  • Be able to follow Incident Management Process.
  • Create and update our Knowledge Management system, SOP’s, and Runbooks.
  • To contribute effectively to organisational and team processes and adhere to policies including but not limited to Data Protection and Information Security
  • Be responsible for ensuring the IT systems and applications are maintained to best practice.
  • Recognise system deficiencies and suggest effective solutions while complying with company policies and procedures.
  • Research current best practice technology solutions and translate them into our environment
  • To participate in Change Advisory Board (CAB) and Problem Management meetings and to share information with the support teams.
  • Be responsible for resolution of all tickets within agreed KPI timelines.
  • Carry out any other duties as required.

Education:

Must hold at least one of the following but all three would be advantageous:

  • Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate AZ-500
  • Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate SC-200
  • Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator Associate SC-300
  • Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate MD-102

PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end-to-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway.


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status.

We are committed to ensuring that individuals with disabilities or access needs are provided with reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, to perform essential job functions, and to access the full benefits of employment. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage.

title: Analyst II, Enhanced Due Diligence
description: Want to help us help others? We’re hiring! GoFundMe is the world’s most powerful community for good, dedicated to helping people help each other. By uniting individuals and nonprofits in one place, GoFundMe makes it easy and safe for people to ask for help and support causes—for themselves and each other. Together, our community has raised more than $40 billion since 2010.Join us! The GoFundMe team is searching for a skilled and passionate Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) Analyst II to join our global Trust & Safety team. Trust & Safety is an integral part of GoFundMe's business. It drives growth, builds and nurtures our brand, and ensures that we always do the right thing by our customers. We are at the front line of GoFundMe’s efforts to protect donors, fundraiser organizers, beneficiaries, and our company from financial crimes and abuse.As a member of the EDD team, you will be responsible for conducting high-quality investigative reviews on high-risk or complex cases. Operating at the front line of GoFundMe’s efforts to protect donors, fundraiser organizers, beneficiaries, and our company from financial crimes and abuse, you will serve as an escalation point for internal teams. In this role, you will perform in-depth EDD analyses, apply judgment to assess risk, and ensure each review is supported by clear, comprehensive, and well-documented findings.The JobLeverage established investigative frameworks to conduct complex EDD investigations on higher-risk customers, fundraisers, and organizations.Own the review of complex cases to assess and mitigate potential financial crime risks, including fraud, AML/CFT concerns, sanctions exposure, adverse media, and politically exposed persons (PEPs).Evaluate fundraiser, account, and transactional activity to identify and stakeholders, balancing risk mitigation with a positive user experience.Develop and maintain subject matter expertise in platform-specific risks, financial crime typologies, and emerging threats.Provide technical guidance and quality feedback to analysts, supporting investigative consistency and adherence to team standards.Drive process improvements and operational initiatives that strengthen the effectiveness and scalability of the EDD program.This position may involve reviewing sensitive material, which can include disturbing images, videos, or text, as well as offensive language and other challenging content (e.g., violence, exploitation, or self-injury). title: IT Business Analyst
description:
Building a sustainable tomorrow

BAM UK & Ireland is recruiting an experienced IT Business Analyst on a 12 month fixed-term contract to support the delivery of high value digital and technology initiatives. Working on a hybrid basis from any of our Ireland or UK locations, you will play a critical role in shaping solutions, enabling effective delivery and ensuring technology adds real, measurable value to our business units.


Your mission
As an IT Business Analyst, you will deliver high quality insights, analysis and support that help our business optimise operations and achieve continuous improvement goals. You will work closely with stakeholders to understand business needs, translate requirements into clear deliverables, and collaborate with delivery teams operating in a hybrid Agile and DevOps environment. Your work will strengthen digital maturity, promote customer centric thinking and ensure predictable, value driven delivery outcomes.

Making Possible

  • Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders through proactive engagement and clear, consistent communication.
  • Facilitate workshops, discovery sessions and cross functional engagement to drive shared understanding and alignment.
  • Shape and refine well defined business problems, opportunities and solution options.
  • Produce clear, unambiguous user stories, requirements and acceptance criteria to support efficient delivery.
  • Develop high quality analysis artefacts such as process maps, data flows, wireframes and mock ups.
  • Collaborate with PMO/Business Owners to maintain a prioritised and transparent backlog.
  • Analyse quantitative and qualitative data to validate business needs, identify trends and strengthen decision making.
  • Ensure solution options align with architecture, technology roadmaps, user experience goals and organisational strategy.
  • Support business cases, including benefits identification, dependency analysis and evidence based recommendations.
  • Work closely with developers, testers and delivery partners to ensure feasibility, clarity and alignment throughout the lifecycle.
  • Support UAT planning and execution, including scenario creation and defect triage.
  • Enable successful change adoption through training materials, user guidance and change readiness support.
  • Validate delivered solutions post implementation and support continuous improvement.
  • Contribute to the promotion of digital maturity, Hybrid/Agile ways of working and collaborative delivery practices.


What’s in it for you?

Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction and civil engineering projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms.
We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with benefits such as:


  • 8% pension contribution
  • Private Health Insurance
  • Dental Plan
  • 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service
  • 2 Wellbeing days annually
  • Paid maternity & paternity leave
  • Learning & Development opportunities - internal progression is encouraged and fostered.
  • Company vehicles (role dependent)
  • .....and many more great perks.

What do you bring to the role?

  • Strong analytical, problem‑solving and critical‑thinking skills.
  • Ability to build positive relationships and communicate effectively with a wide range of stakeholders.
  • Experience in requirements engineering, process modelling, data analysis and Agile documentation.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in cross‑functional teams.
  • Understanding of system interactions, integrations and architectural considerations.
  • Experience facilitating workshops and supporting Agile ceremonies.
  • Experience supporting user testing, solution delivery and change adoption.
  • Experience as a Business Analyst or in a similar role (typically 4+ years).
  • Experience working in Agile environments (Scrum, Kanban) and familiarity with methodologies such as PRINCE2.
  • Experience producing user stories, acceptance criteria and non‑functional requirements.
  • Experience using data to validate problems, quantify benefits and guide decisions.
  • Demonstrated experience developing business cases, user stories, acceptance criteria and non‑functional requirements.
  • Experience in data‑driven analysis to validate problems, quantify benefits and inform recommendations.
  • Experience implementing new systems or supporting process improvements.
  • Experience or professional certifications such as ISEB/BCS, IIBA, Agile BA, Scrum certifications, PRINCE2, Lean Six Sigma or relevant diplomas are desirable (not essential).

If you do not meet every requirement but feel you have the skills and experience to succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply.

About BAM

Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe.

We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social bac

title: Desktop Support Analyst
description:
Applications are invited for the following position:

Title of Post: Desktop Support Analyst

JOB SYNOPSIS
The IT Services team (ITS) is the focal point in ITD for first and second level ICT support to the Faculty, Staff and Students of the University and is a key component in the successful delivery of services by ITD. The ITS team ensures that customer requests are responded to, dealt with and acted on in a timely and professional manner to deliver the highest level of support as required by our customers. The Desktop Support Analyst post is important in helping ITS to deliver many of the day-to-day duties with staff at this level fulfilling important duties such as Service Desk, Workstation support including hardware and software troubleshooting, Installations and general IT support for the Faculty, Staff and Students of the University.

Office of the Director of Information Technology

Information Technology Division

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary Scale: €43,942 - €55,732 p.a.

University of Limerick is a research-led, energetic and enterprising institution with a proud record of innovation and excellence in education and scholarships. We value creativity, curiosity and excellence in our inclusive community which is committed to social good. Our motto Eagna chun Gnímh translates as Wisdom for Action reflecting our mission to contribute to the discovery of new knowledge for a better world.

Informal enquiries regarding the post may be directed to:
Ms Mary O Donoghue
IT Services Manager
Information Technology Division
University of Limerick
Email: mary.c.odonoghue@ul.ie

The closing date for receipt of applications is Friday, 15th May 2026.
Applications must be completed online before 12 noon, Irish Standard Time on the closing date.

Please note your current work authorisation status must allow you to fulfil the terms of the contract on offer.

Please email erecruitment@ul.ie if you experience any difficulties

Further information for applicants and application material is available online from: University of Limerick Vacancies
